predictions.rationale: A dead-heat the market can't split: England 35% vs Argentina 32% with the draw at 33% over 90 minutes. England hold the narrowest edge and the day's news firms it slightly: Rice has shaken off his sickness bug and is expected back in the XI, and Bellingham is incandescent with six tournament goals off back-to-back knockout braces. The defence is still patched (Quansah banned, Henderson out, Konsa at right-back) and Argentina are fully intact with a fit Messi leading the tournament on eight goals, so England edge it on paper but only just, with a shootout firmly on the table.
predictions.disagreement: Tipster consensus leans Argentina against the sharp market: several previews note Argentina are marginally favoured by some books and have never failed to reach a final from a World Cup semi (six from six), citing their intact champion core versus England's thinned-out back line. Sports Mole and others lean 1-2 Argentina.
